q: What is the percentage equivalent of 1/20 + 11/30? (Complete the blank up to two decimal places.)
A bus covers a certain distance at a speed of 72 km/hr and returns the same distance at a speed of 90 km/hr. What is its average speed?
Acid and water are mixed in the ratio 2 : 10 in 540 litres of a mixture. How many litres of acid should be added to this mixture so that the ratio of acid to water becomes 5 : 9?
The ratio of the curved surface area of a right circular cone to its total surface area is 3 : 5. If its slant height is 12.936 cm^2, then what will be its height? (Use pi = 22/7.)

In a competitive examination, a certain piece of work can be completed by P in 9 days and by Q in 18 days. If both of them together can do the work, then in how many days will they together complete the work?
Find that largest number which when each of 45, 95 and 185 is divided by it leaves the same remainder in each case.
Find the compound interest on a sum of Rs.25,200 at an annual rate of 10% for 2 years, when the interest is compounded annually.
The average age of 42 students in a class is 14 years. If the teacher's age is also included, the average age of the group becomes 15 years. What is the teacher's age (in years)?
The average score of a cricketer in 9 innings is 68 runs. If he scores 130 runs in the 10th inning, then by how much will his average score increase?
4 men can complete a certain piece of work in 27 days. How many men will complete the same work in 18 days?

The price of an item increases by 50%, 35% and 50% respectively in three consecutive months, but in the fourth month it decreases by 45%. By what overall percentage did the price of the item increase at the end of these four months? (Round your answer up to two decimal places.)
Rahul's current age is 2/7 of his grandfather's current age. After 16 years, his age will be 2/4 (one half) of his grandfather's age at that time. Find Rahul's grandfather's current age.
The marked price of a television set is Rs.35,000. A merchant gives a discount of 22% and earns a profit of 75%. What is the cost price of the television set?
'A' sells an item bought for Rs.1,50,000 to 'B' at a loss of 30%. Then 'B' sells it to 'C' at a profit of 5%. What is the cost price of the item for 'C'?

The radius of a small sphere is 5 cm, and the radius of a large sphere is made by expanding it in equal proportion to 15 cm. How many times the curved surface area of the small sphere is the curved surface area of the large sphere?

The prices of two types of items P and Q are respectively: Rs.280, Rs.180 and Rs.100. If P and Q items are bought in the ratio 4 : 3 and a total of Rs.27,900 is spent, then how many items of type Q were bought?
A, B and C can each complete a piece of work in 3, 4 and 6 days respectively. If they work together, then in how much time (in days) will they complete the work?
The sum of the digits of a two-digit number is 5. If 9 is added to this number, then their digits get interchanged. Find that number.
Simplify: (x - 3)^2 + (x + 3)^2.
The sides of a triangle are 37 cm, 44 cm and 15 cm. What is the length of its altitude corresponding to the side with the length of 44 cm?
The angle of elevation of the top of a tower from a point 40 m away from the foot of the tower is 45 degrees. Find the height of the tower.
If 20% of a certain number is added to 96, the result is the correct number itself. By what percentage of that number is 65 less than it?
In Bhairav's class there are 17 students placed above him and 17 placed below him. How many students are there in the class?
In the following, a group of letter-number is given in a certain way. Select the option that will logically complete the series. OPD73, RMG70, UJJ67, ?

Two statements followed by two conclusions are given. Assuming the statements to be true even if they seem to differ from commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follows from the statements. Statements: All chargers are batteries. All helmets are chargers. Conclusions: (I) Some chargers are helmets. (II) All batteries are helmets.

Looking at the two given numbers and symbols, answer the question below them. Counting only from the right side end, only those numbers are to be counted. Series: % 7 % 6 % 2 # C $ E O 9 5 6 # 9 & 6 % 7 & (DOG). How many such numbers are there which have exactly one number immediately to their left and one symbol immediately to their right?
In a certain code language, in 'uncle ocean elf', 'so ex rj' is coded; and in 'elf yak queen', 'ku yi ex' is coded. How is the word 'elf' coded in that language?
What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the given number series? 88 75 62 49 36 ?
In a certain way, RVSQ is related to OSPN. In the same way, ZDAY is related to WAXV. Following the same logic, to which of the given options is SWTR related?

Based on the given way, which of the following letter-groups does NOT belong to the same group as the other three? (Note: Operations on letter-groups should be based on their position in the English alphabet; do not consider the structure of the letter-group.)

Six boxes A, B, C, D, E, J, K and others are placed one above the other, but not necessarily in the same order. Box A is placed at the top. There is only one box between A and B. There are as many boxes above box B as below box K. There are two boxes between J and K. How many boxes are placed between B and K?
A letter-number group is given in a certain way. Select the option that will logically complete the series. KR37 MT45 OV53 QX61 ?
In a certain way, QP 7 is related to TO -4. In the same way, AL 14 is related to DK 3. Following the same logic, RX 2 is related to which of the given options?

In the given number series, count from the left end. (All numbers are to be counted singly.) Series: 5 2 1 4 7 8 6 5 2 3 9 1 (DOG). How many such odd numbers are there which have an odd number immediately to their left and an even number immediately to their right?
What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given number series? 7 9 13 19 27 37 ?

From a point A, Samay starts and walks 58 km towards the south direction. Then he turns right and walks 64 km. Then he again turns right and walks 8 km. After that he turns left and walks 5 km. Finally he again turns right and walks. Now in which direction and how far is he from point A? (Note: All movements are made on the ground; until told to stop.)

From the given options, select the set of numbers in which the numbers are related to one another in the same way as the numbers of the given set are related. (Note: Operations on numbers should be done without breaking the numbers into their constituent digits, e.g. operations such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 — operations on 13 can be done; but breaking 13 into 1 and 3 and then doing operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)

O, P, Q, R, W, X and Y are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. R is sitting third to the left of P. There are only two persons between R and X. Q is sitting between W and Y. O is sitting to the immediate right of P. Where is X sitting with respect to Y?

Two statements followed by two conclusions are given. Assuming the statements to be true even if they differ from commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follows from the statements. Statements: All buses are trains. No train is an aeroplane. Conclusions: (I) All buses are trains. (II) No bus is an aeroplane.
In a certain way, EFHG is related to KOBK. In the same way, OLXF is related to KQBK. Following the same logic, to which of the given options is CWJU related?

Read the following statements: 'A + B' means 'A is the father of B'; 'A - B' means 'A is the sister of B'; 'A x B' means 'A is the brother of B'; 'A divided by B' means 'A is the mother of B'. Based on this, in the expression 'M + O divided by Q - H x J', how is M related to J?
